title: Fix MongoDB Projection to Handle Optional Fields with Mixed Types

statement.markdown: You are given a MongoDB query with a projection that needs to handle optional fields with mixed types. The projection should include all fields in the documents, unless the field is an array or an object, in which case it should be excluded. The query also takes as input a list of excluded fields, which should be ignored in the projection.
statement.inputFormat: A JSON object representing the MongoDB query and a list of excluded fields.
statement.outputFormat: A JSON object representing the filtered query with the projection applied.
statement.notes: The query can contain fields of any type, including strings, integers, booleans, arrays, and objects.

constraints.description: Number of documents in the query

examples.explanation: The address field is excluded because it is an object, and the hobbies field is excluded because it is an array.
The interests field is excluded because it is an array.

functionSignature.python: def filter_query(self, query: dict, excluded_fields: list) -> dict:
